{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_7c73c179040942cb92462d51","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→```xml\n     2→<project_specification>\n     3→  <project_name>Image Detection Result Analyzer - Frontend Tool</project_name>\n     4→\n     5→  <overview>\n     6→    Build a comprehensive frontend tool with a Flask backend to analyze the performance of object detection models by comparing ground truth annotations with model predictions. The application will process local datasets consisting of image sequences, ground truth bounding box JSON files, and model prediction JSON files (containing bounding boxes, classes, and confidence scores). It provides two core views: a statistics dashboard displaying per-class detection metrics (recall, false positive rate, etc.) and an image-by-image comparison view showing visual overlays of ground truth and predicted bounding boxes with distinct visual indicators for correct detections, false positives, and missed detections. The tool prioritizes intuitive visualization, accurate metric calculation, and seamless local file handling.\n     7→  </overview>\n     8→\n     9→  <technology_stack>\n    10→    <frontend>\n    11→      <framework>Vanilla HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript (ES6+)</framework>\n    12→      <styling>Custom CSS with Flexbox/Grid, optional Bootstrap 5 (via CDN) for responsive layout</styling>\n    13→      <visualization>Chart.js (CDN) for statistical charts, Canvas API for bounding box rendering</visualization>\n    14→      <file_handling>FileReader API for local file processing</file_handling>\n    15→      <routing>Client-side routing with JavaScript (hash-based) for page navigation</routing>\n    16→      <icons>Font Awesome (CDN) for visual indicators (check, cross, dash)</icons>\n    17→    </frontend>\n    18→    <backend>\n    19→      <runtime>Python 3.8+</runtime>\n    20→      <framework>Flask 2.0+</framework>\n    21→      <data_processing>\n    22→        - JSON parsing with built-in json module\n    23→        - Image processing with Pillow (PIL) for thumbnail generation\n    24→        - Metric calculation (recall, precision, FPR) with numpy\n    25→      </data_processing>\n    26→      <cors>Flask-CORS for cross-origin resource sharing</cors>\n    27→      <file_management>Built-in os/pathlib for local file system interaction</file_management>\n    28→    </backend>\n    29→    <communication>\n    30→      <api>RESTful endpoints for data processing and retrieval</api>\n    31→      <data_format>JSON for all data exchange between frontend and backend</data_format>\n    32→      <file_upload>Multipart/form-data for batch file upload (optional) or local file path specification</file_handling>\n    33→    </communication>\n    34→    <dependencies>\n    35→      <python>\n    36→        - flask>=2.0.0\n    37→        - flask-cors>=3.0.10\n    38→        - pillow>=9.0.0\n    39→        - numpy>=1.21.0\n    40→      </python>\n    41→      <frontend_cdn>\n    42→        - Chart.js v4.4.0\n    43→        - Bootstrap 5.3.0 (optional)\n    44→        - Font Awesome 6.4.0\n    45→      </frontend_cdn>\n    46→    </dependencies>\n    47→  </technology_stack>\n    48→\n    49→  <prerequisites>\n    50→    <environment_setup>\n    51→      - Python 3.8+ installed on the system\n    52→      - Flask and required dependencies installed via pip: pip install flask flask-cors pillow numpy\n    53→      - Local dataset organized in a structured directory (images in /images, ground truth JSON in /gt, predictions JSON in /predictions)\n    54→      - Backend code in /app directory with app.py as entry point\n    55→      - Frontend assets (HTML/CSS/JS) in /app/static directory\n    56→      - Template files in /app/templates directory\n    57→      - Environment variables (optional) for dataset path configuration (.env file with DATASET_PATH)\n    58→    </environment_setup>\n    59→    <dataset_format>\n    60→      <image_sequence>\n    61→        - All images in a single directory (JPG/PNG format) with consistent naming convention (e.g., img_001.jpg, img_002.png)\n    62→        - Image dimensions consistent or variable (tool should handle both)\n    63→      </image_sequence>\n    64→      <ground_truth_json>\n    65→        - Single JSON file or per-image JSON files (naming matching images: img_001.json)\n    66→        - Structure: {\n    67→            \"filename\": \"img_001.jpg\",\n    68→            \"width\": 1920,\n    69→            \"height\": 1080,\n    70→            \"annotations\": [\n    71→              {\n    72→                \"class\": \"car\",\n    73→                \"bbox\": [x1, y1, x2, y2]  # pixel coordinates (top-left, bottom-right)\n    74→              },\n    75→              ...\n    76→            ]\n    77→          }\n    78→      </ground_truth_json>\n    79→      <prediction_json>\n    80→        - Single JSON file or per-image JSON files (naming matching images: img_001_pred.json)\n    81→        - Structure: {\n    82→            \"filename\": \"img_001.jpg\",\n    83→            \"width\": 1920,\n    84→            \"height\": 1080,\n    85→            \"predictions\": [\n    86→              {\n    87→                \"class\": \"car\",\n    88→                \"bbox\": [x1, y1, x2, y2],\n    89→                \"score\": 0.95  # confidence score (0-1)\n    90→              },\n    91→              ...\n    92→            ]\n    93→          }\n    94→      </prediction_json>\n    95→    </dataset_format>\n    96→  </prerequisites>\n    97→\n    98→  <core_features>\n    99→    <file_handling>\n   100→      - Local dataset path configuration (input field for directory path)\n   101→      - Validation of dataset structure (check for images, GT JSON, prediction JSON)\n   102→      - Batch parsing of all files in the dataset directory\n   103→      - Error handling for missing files, invalid JSON formats, and mismatched filenames\n   104→      - Thumbnail generation for all images (150x150px, aspect ratio preserved)\n   105→      - Caching of parsed data to avoid reprocessing on page refresh\n   106→    </file_handling>\n   107→\n   108→    <statistics_dashboard>\n   109→      <metrics_calculation>\n   110→        - **Recall (True Positive Rate)**: TP / (TP + FN) per class\n   111→        - **Precision**: TP / (TP + FP) per class\n   112→        - **False Positive Rate (FPR)**: FP / (FP + TN) per class\n   113→        - **F1 Score**: 2 * (Precision * Recall) / (Precision + Recall) per class\n   114→        - **Overall Accuracy**: (TP + TN) / (TP + TN + FP + FN)\n   115→        - **Average Precision (AP)** per class (optional)\n   116→        - **Intersection over Union (IoU)** threshold configuration (default: 0.5) for TP/FP/FN classification\n   117→      </metrics_calculation>\n   118→      <visualization>\n   119→        - Bar charts for per-class recall, precision, FPR\n   120→        - Pie charts for class distribution in ground truth vs predictions\n   121→        - Summary table with all metrics for easy comparison\n   122→        - Sortable and filterable metric table (by class name, metric value)\n   123→        - Export statistics to CSV/JSON format\n   124→        - Interactive tooltips on charts showing exact metric values\n   125→      </visualization>\n   126→      <user_interface>\n   127→        - Dropdown for IoU threshold adjustment (0.3 to 0.9 in 0.1 increments)\n   128→        - Class filter (show/hide specific classes in charts/tables)\n   129→        - Confidence score threshold filter for predictions (0.0 to 1.0 slider)\n   130→        - Reset filters button to restore default settings\n   131→        - Summary card with key overall metrics (total images, total classes, average recall/precision)\n   132→      </user_interface>\n   133→    </statistics_dashboard>\n   134→\n   135→    <image_comparison_view>\n   136→      <left_panel>\n   137→        - Searchable and filterable file list (by filename, class presence)\n   138→        - Image thumbnails alongside filenames (150x150px)\n   139→        - Scrollable list with fixed height (occupies 100% of viewport height)\n   140→        - Highlight active/selected image in the list\n   141→        - Pagination (optional) for large datasets (10/20/50 images per page)\n   142→        - Class filter (show only images containing specific classes)\n   143→        - Status filter (show only images with FP/FN/perfect detections)\n   144→      </left_panel>\n   145→      <right_panel>\n   146→        <image_display>\n   147→          - High-resolution image view (scaled to fit viewport, maintain aspect ratio)\n   148→          - Interactive canvas overlay for bounding boxes\n   149→          - Bounding box styles:\n   150→            - True Positive (TP): Green border (2px), green checkmark (✓) at top-left corner\n   151→            - False Positive (FP): Red border (2px), red cross (✗) at top-left corner\n   152→            - False Negative (FN): Red border (2px), red horizontal dash (—) at top-left corner\n   153→          - Bounding box labels (class name + confidence score for predictions)\n   154→          - Zoom/pan functionality for detailed inspection\n   155→          - Toggle visibility of ground truth/prediction boxes\n   156→          - Toggle visibility of box labels/indicators\n   157→        </image_display>\n   158→        <per_image_statistics>\n   159→          - Summary card for current image (filename, dimensions, total GT boxes, total predicted boxes)\n   160→          - Per-class breakdown table (GT count, predicted count, TP, FP, FN for each class)\n   161→          - IoU values for each matched bounding box (optional tooltip on hover)\n   162→          - Confidence score distribution for predictions on current image\n   163→          - Navigation buttons (previous/next image) for sequential viewing\n   164→        </per_image_statistics>\n   165→      </right_panel>\n   166→    </image_comparison_view>\n   167→\n   168→    <navigation>\n   169→      - Top navigation bar with links to Statistics Dashboard and Image Comparison View\n   170→      - Active page indicator in navigation bar\n   171→      - Breadcrumb navigation for context (current dataset path, current image)\n   172→      - Back to top button for long pages (statistics dashboard)\n   173→      - Responsive navigation (collapsible menu on mobile devices)\n   174→    </navigation>\n   175→\n   176→    <error_handling>\n   177→      - User-friendly error messages for invalid dataset paths\n   178→      - Validation errors for malformed JSON files (line number + error description)\n   179→      - Warning messages for mismatched filenames (GT/prediction JSON without corresponding image)\n   180→      - Loading spinners during data processing/file parsing\n   181→      - Empty state handling (no data loaded, no images to display)\n   182→      - Fallback UI for unsupported image formats\n   183→    </error_handling>\n   184→\n   185→    <accessibility>\n   186→      - Keyboard navigation (Tab/Enter for list selection, arrow keys for image navigation)\n   187→      - Alt text for all images and icons\n   188→      - High contrast mode for bounding boxes (optional toggle)\n   189→      - Screen reader compatible labels for all UI elements\n   190→      - Responsive design for mobile/tablet/desktop viewing\n   191→      - Text size adjustment option (small/medium/large)\n   192→    </accessibility>\n   193→  </core_features>\n   194→\n   195→  <database_schema>\n   196→    <tables>\n   197→      <dataset_metadata>\n   198→        - id (primary key, integer)\n   199→        - path (text, unique)\n   200→        - total_images (integer)\n   201→        - total_classes (integer)\n   202→        - iou_threshold (float, default 0.5)\n   203→        - confidence_threshold (float, default 0.5)\n   204→        - created_at (timestamp)\n   205→        - last_updated (timestamp)\n   206→      </dataset_metadata>\n   207→\n   208→      <image_metadata>\n   209→        - id (primary key, integer)\n   210→        - dataset_id (foreign key to dataset_metadata.id)\n   211→        - filename (text)\n   212→        - width (integer)\n   213→        - height (integer)\n   214→        - thumbnail_path (text)\n   215→        - total_gt_boxes (integer)\n   216→        - total_pred_boxes (integer)\n   217→        - has_fp (boolean)\n   218→        - has_fn (boolean)\n   219→        - is_perfect (boolean)\n   220→      </image_metadata>\n   221→\n   222→      <classes>\n   223→        - id (primary key, integer)\n   224→        - dataset_id (foreign key to dataset_metadata.id)\n   225→        - name (text, unique per dataset)\n   226→        - total_gt_count (integer)\n   227→        - total_pred_count (integer)\n   228→        - tp_count (integer)\n   229→        - fp_count (integer)\n   230→        - fn_count (integer)\n   231→        - recall (float)\n   232→        - precision (float)\n   233→        - fpr (float)\n   234→        - f1_score (float)\n   235→      </classes>\n   236→\n   237→      <bounding_boxes>\n   238→        - id (primary key, integer)\n   239→        - image_id (foreign key to image_metadata.id)\n   240→        - class_id (foreign key to classes.id)\n   241→        - type (text, enum: 'ground_truth', 'prediction')\n   242→        - x1 (float)\n   243→        - y1 (float)\n   244→        - x2 (float)\n   245→        - y2 (float)\n   246→        - confidence (float, null for ground truth)\n   247→        - iou (float, null for unmatched boxes)\n   248→        - classification (text, enum: 'tp', 'fp', 'fn', null for unclassified)\n   249→      </bounding_boxes>\n   250→    </tables>\n   251→    <notes>\n   252→      - Database is SQLite (file-based) for simplicity, stored as dataset_analysis.db\n   253→      - Data is cleared/overwritten when a new dataset is loaded\n   254→      - Indexes on filename, class name, and image_id for faster queries\n   255→    </notes>\n   256→  </database_schema>\n   257→\n   258→  <api_endpoints_summary>\n   259→    <dataset>\n   260→      - POST /api/dataset/load: Load and parse dataset from provided path\n   261→        - Request body: { \"dataset_path\": \"/path/to/dataset\", \"iou_threshold\": 0.5 }\n   262→        - Response: { \"success\": true, \"dataset_id\": 1, \"total_images\": 100, \"total_classes\": 5, \"errors\": [] }\n   263→      - GET /api/dataset/metadata/:id: Get dataset metadata and summary statistics\n   264→      - DELETE /api/dataset/:id: Clear dataset from memory/database\n   265→    </dataset>\n   266→\n   267→    <statistics>\n   268→      - GET /api/statistics/:dataset_id: Get all per-class statistics\n   269→        - Response: { \"classes\": [...], \"overall_metrics\": {...}, \"iou_threshold\": 0.5 }\n   270→      - POST /api/statistics/recalculate: Recalculate metrics with new thresholds\n   271→        - Request body: { \"dataset_id\": 1, \"iou_threshold\": 0.6, \"confidence_threshold\": 0.7 }\n   272→        - Response: { \"success\": true, \"statistics\": {...} }\n   273→      - GET /api/statistics/export/:dataset_id: Export statistics to CSV/JSON\n   274→        - Query params: { \"format\": \"csv\" }\n   275→    </statistics>\n   276→\n   277→    <images>\n   278→      - GET /api/images/:dataset_id: Get list of all images with metadata (filename, thumbnail path, basic stats)\n   279→        - Query params: { \"page\": 1, \"per_page\": 20, \"class_filter\": \"car\", \"status_filter\": \"fp\" }\n   280→      - GET /api/images/:dataset_id/:image_id: Get detailed data for single image\n   281→        - Response: { \n   282→            \"filename\": \"img_001.jpg\", \n   283→            \"dimensions\": { \"width\": 1920, \"height\": 1080 },\n   284→            \"ground_truth_boxes\": [...],\n   285→            \"prediction_boxes\": [...],\n   286→            \"per_class_stats\": {...},\n   287→            \"thumbnail_path\": \"/static/thumbnails/img_001.jpg\"\n   288→          }\n   289→      - GET /api/images/thumbnail/:dataset_id/:filename: Serve generated thumbnail image\n   290→    </images>\n   291→\n   292→    <classes>\n   293→      - GET /api/classes/:dataset_id: Get list of all classes in dataset with counts\n   294→      - GET /api/classes/:dataset_id/:class_id: Get detailed statistics for single class\n   295→    </classes>\n   296→\n   297→    <health>\n   298→      - GET /api/health: Check server status and version\n   299→        - Response: { \"status\": \"healthy\", \"version\": \"1.0.0\", \"python_version\": \"3.9.7\", \"flask_version\": \"2.2.3\" }\n   300→    </health>\n   301→  </api_endpoints_summary>\n   302→\n   303→  <ui_layout>\n   304→    <main_structure>\n   305→      - Fixed top navigation bar (100% width, 60px height) with project title and page links\n   306→      - Main content area (fills remaining viewport height) with:\n   307→        - Statistics Dashboard: Single column layout with summary cards, charts, and tables\n   308→        - Image Comparison View: Two-column layout (25% left panel, 75% right panel)\n   309→      - Responsive breakpoints:\n   310→        - Mobile (<768px): Image Comparison View switches to single column (list at top, image view below)\n   311→        - The application will process local datasets consisting of image sequences, ground truth bounding box JSON files, and model prediction JSON files (containing bounding boxes, classes, and confidence scores). It provides two core views: a statistics dashboard displaying per-class detection metrics (recall, false positive rate, etc.) and an image-by-image comparison view showing visual overlays of ground truth and predicted bounding boxes with distinct visual indicators for correct detections, false positives, and missed detections. The tool prioritizes intuitive visualization, accurate metric calculation, and seamless local file handling.\n  </overview>\n\n  <technology_stack>\n    <frontend>\n      <framework>Vanilla HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript (ES6+)</framework>\n      <styling>Custom CSS with Flexbox/Grid, optional Bootstrap 5 (via CDN) for responsive layout</styling>\n      <visualization>Chart.js (CDN) for statistical charts, Canvas API for bounding box rendering</visualization>\n      <file_handling>FileReader API for local file processing</file_handling>\n      <routing>Client-side routing with JavaScript (hash-based) for page navigation</routing>\n      <icons>Font Awesome (CDN) for visual indicators (check, cross, dash)</icons>\n    </frontend>\n    <backend>\n      <runtime>Python 3.8+</runtime>\n      <framework>Flask 2.0+</framework>\n      <data_processing>\n        - JSON parsing with built-in json module\n        - Image processing with Pillow (PIL) for thumbnail generation\n        - Metric calculation (recall, precision, FPR) with numpy\n      </data_processing>\n      <cors>Flask-CORS for cross-origin resource sharing</cors>\n      <file_management>Built-in os/pathlib for local file system interaction</file_management>\n    </backend>\n    <communication>\n      <api>RESTful endpoints for data processing and retrieval</api>\n      <data_format>JSON for all data exchange between frontend and backend</data_format>\n      <file_upload>Multipart/form-data for batch file upload (optional) or local file path specification</file_handling>\n    </communication>\n    <dependencies>\n      <python>\n        - flask>=2.0.0\n        - flask-cors>=3.0.10\n        - pillow>=9.0.0\n        - numpy>=1.21.0\n      </python>\n      <frontend_cdn>\n        - Chart.js v4.4.0\n        - Bootstrap 5.3.0 (optional)\n        - Font Awesome 6.4.0\n      </frontend_cdn>\n    </dependencies>\n  </technology_stack>\n\n  <prerequisites>\n    <environment_setup>\n      - Python 3.8+ installed on the system\n      - Flask and required dependencies installed via pip: pip install flask flask-cors pillow numpy\n      - Local dataset organized in a structured directory (images in /images, ground truth JSON in /gt, predictions JSON in /predictions)\n      - Backend code in /app directory with app.py as entry point\n      - Frontend assets (HTML/CSS/JS) in /app/static directory\n      - Template files in /app/templates directory\n      - Environment variables (optional) for dataset path configuration (.env file with DATASET_PATH)\n    </environment_setup>\n    <dataset_format>\n      <image_sequence>\n        - All images in a single directory (JPG/PNG format) with consistent naming convention (e.g., img_001.jpg, img_002.png)\n        - Image dimensions consistent or variable (tool should handle both)\n      </image_sequence>\n      <ground_truth_json>\n        - Single JSON file or per-image JSON files (naming matching images: img_001.json)\n        - Structure: {\n            \"filename\": \"img_001.jpg\",\n            \"width\": 1920,\n            \"height\": 1080,\n            \"annotations\": [\n              {\n                \"class\": \"car\",\n                \"bbox\": [x1, y1, x2, y2]  # pixel coordinates (top-left, bottom-right)\n              },\n              ...\n            ]\n          }\n      </ground_truth_json>\n      <prediction_json>\n        - Single JSON file or per-image JSON files (naming matching images: img_001_pred.json)\n        - Structure: {\n            \"filename\": \"img_001.jpg\",\n            \"width\": 1920,\n            \"height\": 1080,\n            \"predictions\": [\n              {\n                \"class\": \"car\",\n                \"bbox\": [x1, y1, x2, y2],\n                \"score\": 0.95  # confidence score (0-1)\n              },\n              ...\n            ]\n          }\n      </prediction_json>\n    </dataset_format>\n  </prerequisites>\n\n  <core_features>\n    <file_handling>\n      - Local dataset path configuration (input field for directory path)\n      - Validation of dataset structure (check for images, GT JSON, prediction JSON)\n      - Batch parsing of all files in the dataset directory\n      - Error handling for missing files, invalid JSON formats, and mismatched filenames\n      - Thumbnail generation for all images (150x150px, aspect ratio preserved)\n      - Caching of parsed data to avoid reprocessing on page refresh\n    </file_handling>\n\n    <statistics_dashboard>\n      <metrics_calculation>\n        - **Recall (True Positive Rate)**: TP / (TP + FN) per class\n        - **Precision**: TP / (TP + FP) per class\n        - **False Positive Rate (FPR)**: FP / (FP + TN) per class\n        - **F1 Score**: 2 * (Precision * Recall) / (Precision + Recall) per class\n        - **Overall Accuracy**: (TP + TN) / (TP + TN + FP + FN)\n        - **Average Precision (AP)** per class (optional)\n        - **Intersection over Union (IoU)** threshold configuration (default: 0.5) for TP/FP/FN classification\n      </metrics_calculation>\n      <visualization>\n        - Bar charts for per-class recall, precision, FPR\n        - Pie charts for class distribution in ground truth vs predictions\n        - Summary table with all metrics for easy comparison\n        - Sortable and filterable metric table (by class name, metric value)\n        - Export statistics to CSV/JSON format\n        - Interactive tooltips on charts showing exact metric values\n      </visualization>\n      <user_interface>\n        - Dropdown for IoU threshold adjustment (0.3 to 0.9 in 0.1 increments)\n        - Class filter (show/hide specific classes in charts/tables)\n        - Confidence score threshold filter for predictions (0.0 to 1.0 slider)\n        - Reset filters button to restore default settings\n        - Summary card with key overall metrics (total images, total classes, average recall/precision)\n      </user_interface>\n    </statistics_dashboard>\n\n    <image_comparison_view>\n      <left_panel>\n        - Searchable and filterable file list (by filename, class presence)\n        - Image thumbnails alongside filenames (150x150px)\n        - Scrollable list with fixed height (occupies 100% of viewport height)\n        - Highlight active/selected image in the list\n        - Pagination (optional) for large datasets (10/20/50 images per page)\n        - Class filter (show only images containing specific classes)\n        - Status filter (show only images with FP/FN/perfect detections)\n      </left_panel>\n      <right_panel>\n        <image_display>\n          - High-resolution image view (scaled to fit viewport, maintain aspect ratio)\n          - Interactive canvas overlay for bounding boxes\n          - Bounding box styles:\n            - True Positive (TP): Green border (2px), green checkmark (✓) at top-left corner\n            - False Positive (FP): Red border (2px), red cross (✗) at top-left corner\n            - False Negative (FN): Red border (2px), red horizontal dash (—) at top-left corner\n          - Bounding box labels (class name + confidence score for predictions)\n          - Zoom/pan functionality for detailed inspection\n          - Toggle visibility of ground truth/prediction boxes\n          - Toggle visibility of box labels/indicators\n        </image_display>\n        <per_image_statistics>\n          - Summary card for current image (filename, dimensions, total GT boxes, total predicted boxes)\n          - Per-class breakdown table (GT count, predicted count, TP, FP, FN for each class)\n          - IoU values for each matched bounding box (optional tooltip on hover)\n          - Confidence score distribution for predictions on current image\n          - Navigation buttons (previous/next image) for sequential viewing\n        </per_image_statistics>\n      </right_panel>\n    </image_comparison_view>\n\n    <navigation>\n      - Top navigation bar with links to Statistics Dashboard and Image Comparison View\n      - Active page indicator in navigation bar\n      - Breadcrumb navigation for context (current dataset path, current image)\n      - Back to top button for long pages (statistics dashboard)\n      - Responsive navigation (collapsible menu on mobile devices)\n    </navigation>\n\n    <error_handling>\n      - User-friendly error messages for invalid dataset paths\n      - Validation errors for malformed JSON files (line number + error description)\n      - Warning messages for mismatched filenames (GT/prediction JSON without corresponding image)\n      - Loading spinners during data processing/file parsing\n      - Empty state handling (no data loaded, no images to display)\n      - Fallback UI for unsupported image formats\n    </error_handling>\n\n    <accessibility>\n      - Keyboard navigation (Tab/Enter for list selection, arrow keys for image navigation)\n      - Alt text for all images and icons\n      - High contrast mode for bounding boxes (optional toggle)\n      - Screen reader compatible labels for all UI elements\n      - Responsive design for mobile/tablet/desktop viewing\n      - Text size adjustment option (small/medium/large)\n    </accessibility>\n  </core_features>\n\n  <database_schema>\n    <tables>\n      <dataset_metadata>\n        - id (primary key, integer)\n        - path (text, unique)\n        - total_images (integer)\n        - total_classes (integer)\n        - iou_threshold (float, default 0.5)\n        - confidence_threshold (float, default 0.5)\n        - created_at (timestamp)\n        - last_updated (timestamp)\n      </dataset_metadata>\n\n      <image_metadata>\n        - id (primary key, integer)\n        - dataset_id (foreign key to dataset_metadata.id)\n        - filename (text)\n        - width (integer)\n        - height (integer)\n        - thumbnail_path (text)\n        - total_gt_boxes (integer)\n        - total_pred_boxes (integer)\n        - has_fp (boolean)\n        - has_fn (boolean)\n        - is_perfect (boolean)\n      </image_metadata>\n\n      <classes>\n        - id (primary key, integer)\n        - dataset_id (foreign key to dataset_metadata.id)\n        - name (text, unique per dataset)\n        - total_gt_count (integer)\n        - total_pred_count (integer)\n        - tp_count (integer)\n        - fp_count (integer)\n        - fn_count (integer)\n        - recall (float)\n        - precision (float)\n        - fpr (float)\n        - f1_score (float)\n      </classes>\n\n      <bounding_boxes>\n        - id (primary key, integer)\n        - image_id (foreign key to image_metadata.id)\n        - class_id (foreign key to classes.id)\n        - type (text, enum: 'ground_truth', 'prediction')\n        - x1 (float)\n        - y1 (float)\n        - x2 (float)\n        - y2 (float)\n        - confidence (float, null for ground truth)\n        - iou (float, null for unmatched boxes)\n        - classification (text, enum: 'tp', 'fp', 'fn', null for unclassified)\n      </bounding_boxes>\n    </tables>\n    <notes>\n      - Database is SQLite (file-based) for simplicity, stored as dataset_analysis.db\n      - Data is cleared/overwritten when a new dataset is loaded\n      - Indexes on filename, class name, and image_id for faster queries\n    </notes>\n  </database_schema>\n\n  <api_endpoints_summary>\n    <dataset>\n      - POST /api/dataset/load: Load and parse dataset from provided path\n        - Request body: { \"dataset_path\": \"/path/to/dataset\", \"iou_threshold\": 0.5 }\n        - Response: { \"success\": true, \"dataset_id\": 1, \"total_images\": 100, \"total_classes\": 5, \"errors\": [] }\n      - GET /api/dataset/metadata/:id: Get dataset metadata and summary statistics\n      - DELETE /api/dataset/:id: Clear dataset from memory/database\n    </dataset>\n\n    <statistics>\n      - GET /api/statistics/:dataset_id: Get all per-class statistics\n        - Response: { \"classes\": [...], \"overall_metrics\": {...}, \"iou_threshold\": 0.5 }\n      - POST /api/statistics/recalculate: Recalculate metrics with new thresholds\n        - Request body: { \"dataset_id\": 1, \"iou_threshold\": 0.6, \"confidence_threshold\": 0.7 }\n        - Response: { \"success\": true, \"statistics\": {...} }\n      - GET /api/statistics/export/:dataset_id: Export statistics to CSV/JSON\n        - Query params: { \"format\": \"csv\" }\n    </statistics>\n\n    <images>\n      - GET /api/images/:dataset_id: Get list of all images with metadata (filename, thumbnail path, basic stats)\n        - Query params: { \"page\": 1, \"per_page\": 20, \"class_filter\": \"car\", \"status_filter\": \"fp\" }\n      - GET /api/images/:dataset_id/:image_id: Get detailed data for single image\n        - Response: { \n            \"filename\": \"img_001.jpg\", \n            \"dimensions\": { \"width\": 1920, \"height\": 1080 },\n            \"ground_truth_boxes\": [...],\n            \"prediction_boxes\": [...],\n            \"per_class_stats\": {...},\n            \"thumbnail_path\": \"/static/thumbnails/img_001.jpg\"\n          }\n      - GET /api/images/thumbnail/:dataset_id/:filename: Serve generated thumbnail image\n    </images>\n\n    <classes>\n      - GET /api/classes/:dataset_id: Get list of all classes in dataset with counts\n      - GET /api/classes/:dataset_id/:class_id: Get detailed statistics for single class\n    </classes>\n\n    <health>\n      - GET /api/health: Check server status and version\n        - Response: { \"status\": \"healthy\", \"version\": \"1.0.0\", \"python_version\": \"3.9.7\", \"flask_version\": \"2.2.3\" }\n    </health>\n  </api_endpoints_summary>\n\n  <ui_layout>\n    <main_structure>\n      - not (0.0 <= confidence_threshold <= 1.0):\n                return jsonify({\n                    'success': False,\n                    'error': 'confidence_threshold must be between 0.0 and 1.0'\n                }), 400\n        except (ValueError, TypeError):\n            return jsonify({\n                'success': False,\n                'error': 'confidence_threshold must be a valid number'\n            }), 400\n\n        # Load the dataset\n        result = load_dataset(\n            dataset_path=dataset_path,\n            iou_threshold=iou_threshold,\n            confidence_threshold=confidence_threshold\n        )\n\n        if result['success']:\n            return jsonify(result), 200\n        else:\n            return jsonify(result), 400\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        return jsonify({\n            'success': False,\n            'error': str(e)\n        }), 500\n\n\n@app.route('/api/statistics/<int:dataset_id>')\ndef get_statistics_endpoint(dataset_id):\n    \"\"\"\n    API endpoint to retrieve per-class statistics for a dataset\n\n    Args:\n        dataset_id: ID of the dataset to retrieve statistics for\n\n    Returns:\n        JSON response with:\n            - classes (list): Array of class statistics with metrics\n            - overall_metrics (dict): Overall metrics across all classes\n            - iou_threshold (float): IoU threshold used\n            - confidence_threshold (float): Confidence threshold used\n\n    Each class in classes array contains:\n        - id (int): Class ID\n        - name (str): Class name\n        - total_gt_count (int): Number of ground truth boxes\n        - total_pred_count (int): Number of prediction boxes\n        - tp_count (int): Number of true positives\n        - fp_count (int): Number of false positives\n        - fn_count (int): Number of false negatives\n        - recall (float): Recall metric\n        - precision (float): Precision metric\n        - fpr (float): False positive rate\n        - f1_score (float): F1 Score\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        with get_db() as conn:\n            cursor = conn.cursor()\n\n            # Check if dataset exists\n            cursor.execute('''\n                SELECT id, path, total_images, total_classes, iou_threshold, confidence_threshold\n                FROM dataset_metadata\n                WHERE id = ?\n            ''', (dataset_id,))\n\n            dataset_row = cursor.fetchone()\n\n            if dataset_row is None:\n                return jsonify({\n                    'success': False,\n                    'error': f'Dataset with ID {dataset_id} not found'\n                }), 404\n\n            # Get all classes for this dataset\n            cursor.execute('''\n                SELECT id, name, total_gt_count, total_pred_count,\n                       tp_count, fp_count, fn_count,\n                       recall, precision, fpr, f1_score\n                FROM classes\n                WHERE dataset_id = ?\n                ORDER BY name\n            ''', (dataset_id,))\n\n            classes = []\n            for row in cursor.fetchall():\n                classes.append({\n                    'id': row[0],\n                    'name': row[1],\n                    'total_gt_count': row[2],\n                    'total_pred_count': row[3],\n                    'tp_count': row[4],\n                    'fp_count': row[5],\n                    'fn_count': row[6],\n                    'recall': row[7],\n                    'precision': row[8],\n                    'fpr': row[9],\n                    'f1_score': row[10]\n                })\n\n            # Calculate overall metrics\n            total_tp = sum(c['tp_count'] for c in classes)\n            total_fp = sum(c['fp_count'] for c in classes)\n            total_fn = sum(c['fn_count'] for c in classes)\n            total_gt = sum(c['total_gt_count'] for c in classes)\n\n            # Overall recall\n            if (total_tp + total_fn) > 0:\n                overall_recall = total_tp / (total_tp + total_fn)\n            else:\n                overall_recall = 0.0\n\n            # Overall precision\n            if (total_tp + total_fp) > 0:\n                overall_precision = total_tp / (total_tp + total_fp)\n            else:\n                overall_precision = 0.0\n\n            # Overall F1 Score\n            if (overall_precision + overall_recall) > 0:\n                overall_f1 = 2 * (overall_precision * overall_recall) / (overall_precision + overall_recall)\n            else:\n                overall_f1 = 0.0\n\n            # Overall FPR\n            if (total_fp + total_tp) > 0:\n                overall_fpr = total_fp / (total_fp + total_tp)\n            else:\n                overall_fpr = 0.0\n\n            overall_metrics = {\n                'total_gt_boxes': total_gt,\n                'total_pred_boxes': sum(c['total_pred_count'] for c in classes),\n                'total_tp': total_tp,\n                'total_fp': total_fp,\n                'total_fn': total_fn,\n                'recall': overall_recall,\n                'precision': overall_precision,\n                'fpr': overall_fpr,\n                'f1_score': overall_f1\n            }\n\n            return jsonify({\n                'success': True,\n                'classes': classes,\n                'overall_metrics': overall_metrics,\n                'iou_threshold': dataset_row[4],\n                'confidence_threshold': dataset_row[5]\n            }), 200\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        return jsonify({\n            'success': False,\n            'error': str(e)\n        }), 500\n\n\n@app.route('/api/statistics/export/<int:dataset_id>')\ndef               'total_pred_count': row[3],\n                    'tp_count': row[4],\n                    'fp_count': row[5],\n                    'fn_count': row[6],\n                    'recall':  }), 500\n\n\n@app.route('/api/images/<int:dataset_id>')\ndef get_images_endpoint(dataset_id):\n    \"\"\"\n    API endpoint to get paginated list of images for a dataset\n\n    Args:\n        dataset_id: ID of the dataset to retrieve images for\n        page (int, optional): Page number (default: 1)\n        per_page (int, optional): Number of images per page (default: 20)\n        class_filter (str, optional): Filter by class name\n        status_filter (str, optional): Filter by status (fp, fn, perfect)\n\n    Query parameters:\n        page: Page number (default: 1)\n        per_page: Items per page (default: 20, max: 100)\n        class_filter: Only show images containing this class\n        status_filter: Only show images with this status (fp, fn, perfect)\n\n    Returns:\n        JSON response with:\n            - success (bool): Whether request was successful\n            - images (list): Array of image entries\n            - total (int): Total number of images\n            - page (int): Current page number\n            - per_page (int): Items per page\n            - total_pages (int): Total number of pages\n\n    Each image entry contains:\n            - id (int): Image ID\n            - filename (str): Image filename\n            - width (int): Image width\n            - height (int): Image height\n            - thumbnail_path (str): Path to thumbnail\n            - total_gt_boxes (int): Number of GT boxes\n            - total_pred_boxes (int): Number of prediction boxes\n            - has_fp (bool): Has false positives\n            - has_fn (bool): Has false negatives\n            - is_perfect (bool): Perfect detection (no FP or FN)\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Get query parameters\n        page = request.args.get('page', 1, type=int)\n        per_page = request.args.get('per_page', 20, type=int)\n        class_filter = request.args.get('class_filter')\n        status_filter = request.args.get('status_filter')\n\n        # Validate parameters\n        if page < 1:\n            return jsonify({\n                'success': False,\n                'error': 'page must be a positive integer'\n            }), 400\n\n        if per_page < 1 or per_page > 100:\n            return jsonify({\n                'success': False,\n                'error': 'per_page must be between 1 and 100'\n            }), 400\n\n        # Validate status_filter\n        valid_status_filters = ['fp', 'fn', 'perfect']\n        if status_filter is not None and status_filter not in valid_status_filters:\n            return jsonify({\n                'success': False,\n                'error': f'status_filter must be one of: {\", \".join(valid_status_filters)}'\n            }), 400\n\n        with get_db() as conn:\n            cursor = conn.cursor()\n\n            # Check if dataset exists\n            cursor.execute('''\n                SELECT id FROM dataset_metadata WHERE id = ?\n            ''', (dataset_id,))\n\n            if cursor.fetchone() is None:\n                return jsonify({\n                    'success': False,\n                    'error': f'Dataset with ID {dataset_id} not found'\n                }), 404\n\n            # Build base query with filters\n            base_query = '''\n                SELECT id, filename, width, height, thumbnail_path,\n                       total_gt_boxes, total_pred_boxes, has_fp, has_fn, is_perfect\n                FROM image_metadata\n                WHERE dataset_id = ?\n            '''\n            query_params = [dataset_id]\n\n            # Add class filter if provided\n            if class_filter:\n                base_query += '''\n                    AND id IN (\n                        SELECT im.id FROM image_metadata im\n                        JOIN bounding_boxes bb ON bb.image_id = im.id\n                        JOIN classes c ON bb.class_id = c.id\n                        WHERE im.dataset_id = ? AND c.name = ? AND bb.type = 'ground_truth'\n                    )\n                '''\n                query_params.append(dataset_id)\n                query_params.append(class_filter)\n\n            # Add status filter if provided\n            if status_filter == 'fp':\n                base_query += ' AND has_fp = 1'\n            elif status_filter == 'fn':\n                base_query += ' AND has_fn = 1'\n            elif status_filter == 'perfect':\n                base_query += ' AND is_perfect = 1'\n\n            # Add ordering and pagination\n            base_query += ' ORDER BY id'\n            base_query += ' LIMIT ? OFFSET ?'\n            offset = (page - 1) * per_page\n            query_params.extend([per_page, offset])\n\n            # Execute query to get images\n            cursor.execute(base_query, query_params)\n\n            # Fetch images before executing count query\n            images = []\n            for row in cursor.fetchall():\n                images.append({\n                    'id': row[0],\n                    'filename': row[1],\n                    'width': row[2],\n                    'height': row[3],\n                    'thumbnail_path': row[4],\n                    'total_gt_boxes': row[5],\n                    'total_pred_boxes': row[6],\n                    'has_fp': bool(row[7]),\n                    'has_fn': bool(row[8]),\n                    'is_perfect': bool(row[9])\n                })\n\n            # Get total count\n            count_query = '''\n                SELECT COUNT(*) FROM image_metadata WHERE dataset_id = ?\n            '''\n            count_params = [dataset_id]\n\n            if class_filter:\n                count_query += '''\n                    AND id IN (\n                        SELECT im.id FROM image_metadata im\n                        JOIN bounding_boxes bb ON bb.image_id = im.id\n                        JOIN classes c ON bb.class_id = c.id\n                        WHERE im.dataset_id = ? AND c.name = ? AND bb.type = 'ground_truth'\n                    )\n                '''\n                count_params.append(dataset_id)\n                count_params.append(class_filter)\n\n            if status_filter == 'fp':\n                count_query += ' AND has_fp = 1'\n            elif status_filter == 'fn':\n                count_query += ' AND has_fn = 1'\n            elif status_filter == 'perfect':\n                count_query += ' AND is_perfect = 1'\n\n            cursor.execute(count_query, count_params)\n            total_count = cursor.fetchone()[0]\n\n            # Calculate total pages\n            total_pages = (total_count + per_page - 1) // per_page if total_count > 0 else 1\n\n            return jsonify({\n                'success': True,\n                'images': images,\n                'total': total_count,\n                'page': page,\n                'per_page': per_page,\n                'total_pages': total_pages\n            }), 200\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        return jsonify({\n            'success': False,\n            'error': str(e)\n        }), 500\n\n\n@app.route('/api/images/<int:dataset_id>/<int:image_id>')\ndef get_image_detail_endpoint(dataset_id, image_id):\n    \"\"\"\n    API endpoint to get detailed data for a single image\n\n    Args:\n        dataset_id: ID of dataset\n        image_id: ID of image to retrieve details for\n\n    Returns:\n        JSON response with:\n            - success (bool): Whether request was successful\n            - filename (str): Image filename\n            - dimensions (dict): Image width and height\n            - ground_truth_boxes (list): Array of ground truth bounding boxes\n            - prediction_boxes (list): Array of prediction bounding boxes\n            - per_class_stats (dict): Statistics breakdown by class\n            - thumbnail_path (str): Path to thumbnail\n\n    Each box in ground_truth_boxes and prediction_boxes contains:\n        - id (int): Box ID\n        - class_name (str): Class name\n        - bbox (list): [x1, y1, x2, y2] coordinates\n        - confidence (float): Confidence score (predictions only)\n        - iou (float): IoU value (for matched boxes only)\n        - classification (str): 'tp', 'fp', 'fn', or null\n\n    Each entry in per_class_stats contains:\n        - gt_count (int): Number of ground truth boxes\n        - pred_count (int): Number of prediction boxes\n        - tp (int): True positives\n        - fp (int): False positives\n        - fn (int): False negatives\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        with get_db() as conn:\n            cursor = conn.cursor()\n\n            # Check if dataset exists\n            cursor.execute('''\n                SELECT id FROM dataset_metadata WHERE id = ?\n            ''', (dataset_id,))\n\n            if cursor.fetchone() is None:\n                return jsonify({\n                    'success': False,\n                    'error': f'Dataset with ID {dataset_id} not found'\n                }), 404\n\n            # Get image metadata\n            cursor.execute('''\n                SELECT id, filename, width, height, thumbnail_path,\n                       total_gt_boxes, total_pred_boxes\n                FROM image_metadata\n                WHERE id = ? AND dataset_id = ?\n            ''', (image_id, dataset_id))\n\n            image_row = cursor.fetchone()\n\n            if image_row is None:\n                return jsonify({\n                    'success': False,\n                    'error': f'Image with ID {image_id} not found in dataset {dataset_id}'\n                }), 404\n\n            # Get all bounding boxes for this image\n            cursor.execute('''\n                SELECT bb.id, bb.class_id, bb.type, bb.x1, bb.y1, bb.x2, bb.y2,\n                       bb.confidence, bb.iou, bb.classification, c.name\n                FROM bounding_boxes bb\n                JOIN classes c ON bb.class_id = c.id\n                WHERE bb.image_id = ?\n                ORDER BY bb.id\n            ''', (image_id,))\n\n            bbox_rows = cursor.fetchall()\n\n            # Separate ground truth and prediction boxes\n            ground_truth_boxes = []\n            prediction_boxes = []\n\n            for row in bbox_rows:\n                (bbox_id, class_id, bbox_type, x1, y1, x2, y2,\n                 confidence, iou, classification, class_name) = row\n\n                box_data = {\n                    'id': bbox_id,\n                    'class_name': class_name,\n                    'bbox': [x1, y1, x2, y2],\n                    'iou': iou,\n                    'classification': classification\n                }\n\n                if bbox_type == 'ground_truth':\n                    ground_truth_boxes.append(box_data)\n                elif bbox_type == 'prediction':\n                    box_data['confidence'] = confidence\n                    prediction_boxes.append(box_data)\n\n            # Calculate per-class statistics for this image\n            cursor.execute('''\n                SELECT c.name, bb.type, bb.classification\n                FROM bounding_boxes bb\n                JOIN classes c ON bb.class_id = c.id\n                WHERE bb.image_id = ?\n            ''', (image_id,))\n\n            class_rows = cursor.fetchall()\n\n            per_class_stats = {}\n\n            for class_name, bbox_type, classification in class_rows:\n                if class_name not in per_class_stats:\n                    per_class_stats[class_name] = {\n                        'gt_count': 0,\n                        'pred_count': 0,\n                        'tp': 0,\n                        'fp': 0,\n                        'fn': 0\n                    }\n\n                stats = per_class_stats[class_name]\n\n                if bbox_type == 'ground_truth':\n                    stats['gt_count'] += 1\n                    if classification == 'fn':\n                        stats['fn'] += 1\n                    elif classification == 'tp':\n                        stats['tp'] += 1\n                elif bbox_type == 'prediction':\n                    stats['pred_count'] += 1\n                    if classification == 'fp':\n                        stats['fp'] += 1\n\n            return jsonify({\n                'success': True,\n                'filename': image_row[1],\n                'dimensions': {\n                    'width': image_row[2],\n                    'height': image_row[3]\n                },\n                'ground_truth_boxes': ground_truth_boxes,\n                'prediction_boxes': prediction_boxes,\n                'per_class_stats': per_class_stats,\n                'thumbnail_path': image_row[4]\n            }), 200\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        return jsonify({\n            'success': False,\n            'error':       }\n   267→\n   268→        container.innerHTML = filteredImages.map(image => {\n   269→            const statusClass = image.is_perfect ? 'status-perfect' : (image.has_fp || image.has_fn ? 'status-warning' : 'status-perfect');\n   270→            return `\n   271→                <div class=\"image-list-item\" data-image-id=\"${image.id}\">\n   272→                    <img src=\"${image.thumbnail_path || ''}\" alt=\"${this.escapeHtml(image.filename)}\" class=\"thumbnail\" onerror=\"this.src='data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg xmlns=%22http://www.w3.org/2000/svg%22 width=%2260%22 height=%2260%22%3E%3Crect fill=%22%23e2e8f0%22 width=%22100%25%22 height=%22100%25%22/%3E%3Ctext fill=%22%2394a3b8%22 x=%2250%25%22 y=%2250%25%22 text-anchor=%22middle%22 dy=%22.3em%22%3E%3F%3C/text%3E%3C/svg%3E'\">\n   273→                    <div class=\"image-info\">\n   274→                        <div class=\"filename\">${this.escapeHtml(image.filename)}</div>\n   275→                        <div class=\"image-stats\">\n   276→                            GT: ${image.total_gt_boxes} | Pred: ${image.total_pred_boxes}\n   277→                        </div>\n   278→                    </div>\n   279→                    <span class=\"status-dot ${statusClass}\" title=\"${image.is_perfect ? 'Perfect' : (image.has_fp || image.has_fn ? 'Has errors' : 'Good')}\"></span>\n   280→                </div>\n   281→            `;\n   282→        }).join('');\n   283→\n   284→        // Add click event listeners to image list items\n   285→        container.querySelectorAll('.image-list-item').forEach(item => {\n   286→            item.addEventListener('click', () => {\n   287→                const imageId = parseInt(item.getAttribute('data-image-id'));\n   288→                this.selectImage(imageId);\n   289→            });\n   290→        });\n   291→    }\n   292→\n   293→    async selectImage(imageId) {\n   294→        if (!this.datasetId) return;\n   295→\n   296→        this.currentImageId = imageId;\n   297→\n   298→        // Update active state in list\n   299→        document.querySelectorAll('.image-list-item').forEach(item => {\n   300→            item.classList.remove('active');\n   301→            if (parseInt(item.getAttribute('data-image-id')) === imageId) {\n   302→                item.classList.add('active');\n   303→            }\n   304→        });\n   305→\n   306→        // Load image details\n   307→        await this.loadImageDetail(imageId);\n   308→    }\n   309→\n   310→    async loadImageDetail(imageId) {\n   311→        if (!this.datasetId) return;\n   312→\n   313→        const detailContent = document.getElementById('image-detail-content');\n   314→        if (detailContent) {\n   315→            detailContent.innerHTML = '<div class=\"text-center py-5\"><i class=\"fas fa-spinner fa-spin fa-2x mb-3\"></i><p>Loading image details...</p></div>';\n   316→        }\n   317→\n   318→        try {\n   319→            const response = await fetch(`/api/images/${this.datasetId}/${imageId}`);\n   320→            const data = await response.json();\n   321→\n   322→            if (data.success) {\n   323→                this.updateImageDetail(data);\n   324→            }\n   325→        } catch (error) {\n   326→            console.error('Error loading image detail:', error);\n   327→            if (detailContent) {\n   328→                detailContent.innerHTML = '<div class=\"text-center text-danger py-5\">Failed to load image details</div>';\n   329→            }\n   330→        }\n   331→    }\n   332→\n   333→    updateImageDetail(data) {\n   334→        const detailContent = document.getElementById('image-detail-content');\n   335→        if (!detailContent) return;\n   336→\n   337→        // Calculate total boxes\n   338→        const totalGT = data.ground_truth_boxes ? data.ground_truth_boxes.length : 0;\n   339→        const totalPred = data.prediction_boxes ? data.prediction_boxes.length : 0;\n   340→\n   341→        // Build per-class stats table rows\n   342→        const classStatsRows = Object.entries(data.per_class_stats || {}).map(([className, stats]) => `\n   343→            <tr>\n   344→                <td>${this.escapeHtml(className)}</td>\n   345→                <td class=\"text-center\">${stats.gt_count || 0}</td>\n   346→                <td class=\"text-center\">${stats.pred_count || 0}</td>\n   347→                <td class=\"text-center\">${stats.tp || 0}</td>\n   348→                <td class=\"text-center\">${stats.fp || 0}</td>\n   349→                <td class=\"text-center\">${stats.fn || 0}</td>\n   350→            </tr>\n   351→        `).join('');\n   352→\n   353→        detailContent.innerHTML = `\n   354→            <div class=\"image-header-section\">\n   355→                <div class=\"d-flex justify-content-between align-items-center mb-3\">\n   356→                    <div>\n   357→                        <h5 class=\"mb-1\"><i class=\"fas fa-image me-2\"></i>${this.escapeHtml(data.filename)}</h5>\n   358→                        <p class=\"mb-0 text-muted\">\n   359→                            <small>${data.dimensions ? `${data.dimensions.width} x ${data.dimensions.height} pixels` : ''}</small>\n   360→                        </p>\n   361→                    </div>\n   362→                    <div class=\"d-flex gap-2\">\n   363→                        <button class=\"btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm\" id=\"prev-image-btn\">\n   364→                            <i class=\"fas fa-chevron-left me-1\"></i> Previous\n   365→                        </button>\n   366→                        <button class=\"btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm\" id=\"next-image-btn\">\n   367→                            Next <i class=\"fas fa-chevron-right ms-1\"></i>\n   368→                        </button>\n   369→                    </div>\n   370→                </div>\n   371→                <div class=\"d-flex gap-3\">\n   372→                    <span class=\"badge bg-primary\">GT: ${totalGT}</span>\n   373→                    <span class=\"badge bg-info\">Pred: ${totalPred}</span>\n   374→                </div>\n   375→\n   376→                <!-- Box Visibility Toggles -->\n   377→                <div class=\"d-flex gap-2 mb-3 mt-3\">\n   378→                    <button class=\"btn btn-sm toggle-btn active\" id=\"toggle-gt-boxes\" title=\"Show/Hide Ground Truth Boxes\">\n   379→                        <i class=\"fas fa-check-square me-1\"></i> GT Boxes\n   380→                    </button>\n   381→                    <button class=\"btn btn-sm toggle-btn active\" id=\"toggle-pred-boxes\" title=\"Show/Hide Prediction Boxes\">\n   382→                        <i class=\"fas fa-crosshairs me-1\"></i> Pred Boxes\n   383→                    </button>\n   384→                    <button class=\"btn btn-sm toggle-btn active\" id=\"toggle-labels\" title=\"Show/Hide Labels\">\n   385→                        <i class=\"fas fa-tag me-1\"></i> Labels\n   386→                    </button>\n   387→                </div>\n   388→\n   389→                <!-- Zoom Controls -->\n   390→                <div class=\"d-flex gap-2 mb-3\">\n   391→                    <button class=\"btn btn-sm zoom-btn\" id=\"zoom-out\" title=\"Zoom Out\">\n   392→                        <i class=\"fas fa-search-minus\"></i>\n   393→                    </button>\n   394→                    <button class=\"btn btn-sm zoom-btn\" id=\"zoom-reset\" title=\"Reset Zoom\">\n   395→                        <span id=\"zoom-level-display\">100%</span>\n   396→                    </button>\n   397→                    <button class=\"btn btn-sm zoom-btn\" id=\"zoom-in\" title=\"Zoom In\">\n   398→                        <i class=\"fas fa-search-plus\"></i>\n   399→                    </button>\n   400→                </div>\n   401→            </div>\n   402→\n   403→            <div class=\"image-display-section\">\n   404→                <div class=\"card\">\n   405→                    <div class=\"card-body p-0\">\n   406→                        <div class=\"canvas-container\" id=\"canvas-container\">\n   407→                            <canvas id=\"image-canvas\"></canvas>\n   408→                        </div>\n   409→                    </div>\n   410→                </div>\n   411→            </div>\n   412→\n   413→            <div class=\"image-statistics-section\">\n   414→                <div class=\"card\">\n   415→                    <div class=\"card-header\">\n   416→                        <h6 class=\"mb-0\"><i class=\"fas fa-chart-bar me-2\"></i>Per-Image Statistics</h6>\n   417→                    </div>\n   418→                    <div class=\"card-body\">\n   419→                        <div class=\"table-responsive\">\n   420→                            <table class=\"table table-bordered table-striped\">\n   421→                                <thead class=\"table-light\">\n   422→                                    <tr>\n   423→                                        <th>Class</th>\n   424→                                        <th class=\"text-center\">GT</th>\n   425→                                        <th class=\"text-center\">Pred</th>\n   426→                                        <th class=\"text-center\">TP</th>\n   427→                                        <th class=\"text-center\">FP</th>\n   428→                                        <th class=\"text-center\">FN</th>\n   429→                                    </tr>\n   430→                                </thead>\n   431→                                <tbody>\n   432→                                    ${classStatsRows || '<tr><td colspan=\"6\" class=\"text-center text-muted\">No statistics available</td></tr>'}\n   433→                                </tbody>\n   434→                            </table>\n   435→                        </div>\n   436→                    </div>\n   437→                </div>\n   438→            </div>\n   439→        `;\n   440→\n   441→        // Add event listeners for navigation buttons\n   442→        const prevBtn = document.getElementById('prev-image-btn');\n   443→        const nextBtn = document.getElementById('next-image-btn');\n   444→        if (prevBtn) {\n   445→            prevBtn.addEventListener('click', () => this.navigateImage(-1));\n   446→        }\n   447→        if (nextBtn) {\n   448→            nextBtn.addEventListener('click', () => this.navigateImage(1));\n   449→        }\n   450→\n   451→        // Store current image data for re-rendering\n   452→        this.currentImageData = data;\n   453→\n   454→        // Render image with bounding boxes\n   455→        this.renderImageWithBoundingBoxes(data);\n   456→\n   457→        // Add event listeners for toggle buttons\n   458→        this.setupToggleButtons();\n   459→\n   460→        // Add event listeners for zoom controls\n   461→        this.setupZoomControls();\n   462→\n   463→        // Add event listeners for pan/drag functionality\n   464→        this.setupPanDrag();\n   465→    }\n   466→\n   467→    renderImageWithBoundingBoxes(data) {\n   468→        const canvas = document.getElementById('image-canvas');\n   469→        const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');\n   470→        if (!canvas || !ctx) return;\n   471→\n   472→        // Set canvas dimensions\n   473→        const width = data.dimensions?.width || 800;\n   474→        const height = data.dimensions?.height || 600;\n   475→        canvas.width = width;\n   476→        canvas.height = height;\n   477→\n   478→        // Clear canvas and apply zoom/pan transforms\n   479→        ctx.setTransform(1, 0, 0, 1, 0, 0);\n   480→        ctx.clearRect(0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height);\n   481→\n   482→        // Apply zoom and pan transformations\n   483→        ctx.translate(this.panX, this.panY);\n   484→        ctx.scale(this.zoomLevel, this.zoomLevel);\n   485→\n   486→        // Create a placeholder image (since we don't have actual image files)\n   487→        // In a real implementation, you would load the actual image file\n   488→        this.drawPlaceholderImage(ctx, width, height);\n   489→\n   490→        // Draw bounding boxes\n   491→        const gtBoxes = data.ground_truth_boxes || [];\n   492→        const predBoxes = data.prediction_boxes || [];\n   493→\n   494→        // Combine all boxes for rendering\n   495→        // FN boxes are GT boxes without matching prediction (classification is null)\n   496→        const fnBoxes = gtBoxes.filter(box => box.classification === null);\n   497→        // FP boxes are prediction boxes without matching GT\n   498→        const fpBoxes = predBoxes.filter(box => box.classification === 'fp');\n   499→        // TP boxes are matched predictions (classification is 'tp')\n   500→        const tpBoxes = predBoxes.filter(box => box.classification === 'tp');\n   501→\n   502→        // Draw GT boxes (FN only, since TP are predictions that match GT)\n   503→        if (this.showGTBoxes) {\n   504→            fnBoxes.forEach(box => this.drawBoundingBox(ctx, box, 'fn'));\n   505→        }\n   506→\n   507→        // Draw prediction boxes (FP and TP)\n   508→        if (this.showPredBoxes) {\n   509→            fpBoxes.forEach(box => this.drawBoundingBox(ctx, box, 'fp'));\n   510→            tpBoxes.forEach(box => this.drawBoundingBox(ctx, box, 'tp'));\n   511→        }\n   512→    }\n   513→\n   514→    drawPlaceholderImage(ctx, width, height) {\n   515→        // Draw a gradient background\n   516→        const gradient = ctx.createLinearGradient(0, 0, width, height);\n   517→        gradient.addColorStop(0, '#f0f9ff');\n   518→        gradient.addColorStop(1, '#e0f2fe');\n   519→        ctx.fillStyle = gradient;\n   520→        ctx.fillRect(0, 0, width, height);\n   521→\n   522→        // Draw some placeholder shapes to simulate an image\n   523→        ctx.strokeStyle = '#cbd5e1';\n   524→        ctx.lineWidth = 1;\n   525→\n   526→        // Draw a grid\n   527→        for (let x = 0; x < width; x += 50) {\n   528→            ctx.beginPath();\n   529→            ctx.moveTo(x, 0);\n   530→            ctx.lineTo(x, height);\n   531→            ctx.stroke();\n   532→        }\n   533→        for (let y = 0; y < height; y += 50) {\n   534→            ctx.beginPath();\n   535→            ctx.moveTo(0, y);\n   536→            ctx.lineTo(width, y);\n   537→            ctx.stroke();\n   538→        }\n   539→\n   540→        // Draw placeholder text\n   541→        ctx.fillStyle = '#94a3b8';\n   542→        ctx.font = '24px Inter, sans-serif';\n   543→        ctx.textAlign = 'center';\n   544→        ctx.textBaseline = 'middle';\n   545→        ctx.fillText('Image Placeholder', width / 2, height / 2);\n   546→        ctx.font = '14px Inter, sans-serif';\n   547→        ctx.fillText(`${width} x ${height}`, width / 2, height / 2 + 30);\n   548→    }\n   549→\n   550→    drawBoundingBox(ctx, box, type) {\n   551→        const bbox = box.bbox;\n   552→        if (!bbox || bbox.length !== 4) return;\n   553→\n   554→        const [x1, y1, x2, y2] = bbox;\n   555→        const width = x2 - x1;\n   556→        const height = y2 - y1;\n   557→\n   558→        // Set style based on type\n   559→        let color, lineWidth;\n   560→        switch (type) {\n   561→            case 'tp':\n   562→                color = '#22c55e'; // green\n   563→                lineWidth = 2;\n   564→                break;\n   565→            case 'fp':\n   566→                color = '#ef4444'; // red\n   567→                lineWidth = 2;\n   568→                break;\n   569→            case 'fn':\n   570→                color = '#ef4444'; // red\n   571→                lineWidth = 2;\n   572→                break;\n   573→            default:\n   574→                return;\n   575→        }\n   576→\n   577→        // Draw bounding box\n   578→        ctx.strokeStyle = color;\n   579→        ctx.lineWidth = lineWidth;\n   580→\n   581→        if (type === 'fn') {\n   582→            ctx.setLineDash([5, 3]); // Dashed line for FN\n   583→        } else {\n   584→            ctx.setLineDash([]);\n   585→        }\n   586→\n   587→        ctx.strokeRect(x1, y1, width, height);\n   588→        ctx.setLineDash([]); // Reset dash\n   589→\n   590→        // Draw corner indicator (24x24px square at top-left)\n   591→        const indicatorSize = 24;\n   592→        const indicatorX = x1 - 2;\n   593→        const indicatorY = y1 - 12;\n   594→        const centerX = indicatorX + indicatorSize / 2;\n   595→        const centerY = indicatorY + indicatorSize / 2;\n   596→\n   597→        ctx.fillStyle = color;\n   598→        ctx.fillRect(indicatorX, indicatorY, indicatorSize, indicatorSize);\n   599→\n   600→        // Draw icon in the corner indicator using canvas paths instead of text\n   601→        ctx.fillStyle = 'white';\n   602→        ctx.strokeStyle = 'white';\n   603→        ctx.lineWidth = 2;\n   604→\n   605→        if (type === 'tp') {\n   606→            // Draw checkmark (✓)\n   607→            ctx.beginPath();\n   608→            ctx.moveTo(centerX - 4, centerY);\n   609→            ctx.lineTo(centerX, centerY + 4);\n   610→            ctx.lineTo(centerX + 6, centerY - 6);\n   611→            ctx.stroke();\n   612→        } else if (type === 'fp') {\n   613→            // Draw cross (✗)\n   614→            ctx.beginPath();\n   615→            ctx.moveTo(centerX - 4, centerY - 4);\n   616→            ctx.lineTo(centerX + 4, centerY + 4);\n   617→            ctx.moveTo(centerX + 4, centerY - 4);\n   618→            ctx.lineTo(centerX - 4, centerY + 4);\n   619→            ctx.stroke();\n   620→        } else if (type === 'fn') {\n   621→            // Draw dash (—)\n   622→            ctx.beginPath();\n   623→            ctx.moveTo(centerX - 6, centerY);\n   624→            ctx.lineTo(centerX + 6, centerY);\n   625→            ctx.stroke();\n   626→        }\n   627→\n   628→        // Draw label (class name and confidence for predictions)\n   629→        if (this.showLabels) {\n   630→            let labelText = '';\n   631→            if (box.class_name) {\n   632→                labelText = box.class_name;\n   633→            }\n   634→            if (box.confidence !== undefined && box.confidence !== null) {\n   635→                labelText += ` (${box.confidence.toFixed(2)})`;\n   636→            }\n   637→\n   638→            if (labelText) {\n   639→                ctx.font = '11px Inter, sans-serif';\n   640→                const textMetrics = ctx.measureText(labelText);\n   641→                const labelWidth = textMetrics.width + 12;\n   642→                const labelHeight = 20;\n   643→\n   644→                // Draw label background (semi-transparent)\n   645→                ctx.fillStyle = 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.7)';\n   646→                ctx.fillRect(x1, y1 + 12, labelWidth, labelHeight);\n   647→\n   648→                // Draw label text\n   649→                ctx.fillStyle = 'white';\n   650→                ctx.textAlign = 'left';\n   651→                ctx.textBaseline = 'middle';\n   652→                ctx.fillText(labelText, x1 + 6, y1 + 22);\n   653→            }\n   654→        }\n   655→    }\n   656→\n   657→    setupToggleButtons() {\n   658→        // GT Boxes toggle\n   659→        const gtToggle = document.getElementById('toggle-gt-boxes');\n   660→        if (gtToggle) {\n   661→            gtToggle.addEventListener('click', () => {\n   662→                this.showGTBoxes = !this.showGTBoxes;\n   663→                gtToggle.classList.toggle('active');\n   664→                this.reRenderCurrentImage();\n   665→            });\n   666→        }\n   667→\n   668→        // Prediction Boxes toggle\n   669→        const predToggle = document.getElementById('toggle-pred-boxes');\n   670→        if (predToggle) {\n   671→            predToggle.addEventListener('click', () => {\n   672→                this.showPredBoxes = !this.showPredBoxes;\n   673→                predToggle.classList.toggle('active');\n   674→                this.reRenderCurrentImage();\n   675→            });\n   676→        }\n   677→\n   678→        // Labels toggle\n   679→        const labelsToggle = document.getElementById('toggle-labels');\n   680→        if (labelsToggle) {\n   681→            labelsToggle.addEventListener('click', () => {\n   682→                this.showLabels = !this.showLabels;\n   683→                labelsToggle.classList.toggle('active');\n   684→                this.reRenderCurrentImage();\n   685→            });\n   686→        }\n   687→    }\n   688→\n   689→    reRenderCurrentImage() {\n   690→        // Re-render the current image with updated visibility settings\n   691→        if (this.currentImageData) {\n   692→            this.renderImageWithBoundingBoxes(this.currentImageData);\n   693→        }\n   694→    }\n   695→\n   696→    setupZoomControls() {\n   697→        // Zoom in button\n   698→        const zoomInBtn = document.getElementById('zoom-in');\n   699→        if (zoomInBtn) {\n   700→            zoomInBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {\n   701→                this.zoomLevel = Math.min(this.zoomLevel + 0.25, 5.0); // Max zoom 5x\n   702→                this.updateZoomLevelDisplay();\n   703→                this.updateCanvasCursor();\n   704→                this.reRenderCurrentImage();\n   705→            });\n   706→        }\n   707→\n   708→        // Zoom out button\n   709→        const zoomOutBtn = document.getElementById('zoom-out');\n   710→        if (zoomOutBtn) {\n   711→            zoomOutBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {\n   712→                this.zoomLevel = Math.max(this.zoomLevel - 0.25, 0.25); // Min zoom 0.25x\n   713→                this.updateZoomLevelDisplay();\n   714→                this.updateCanvasCursor();\n   715→                this.reRenderCurrentImage();\n   716→            });\n   717→        }\n   718→\n   719→        // Reset zoom button\n   720→        const zoomResetBtn = document.getElementById('zoom-reset');\n   721→        if (zoomResetBtn) {\n   722→            zoomResetBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {\n   723→                this.zoomLevel = 1.0;\n   724→                this.panX = 0;\n   725→                this.panY = 0;\n   726→                this.updateZoomLevelDisplay();\n   727→                this.updateCanvasCursor();\n   728→                this.reRenderCurrentImage();\n   729→            });\n   730→        }\n   731→    }\n   732→\n   733→    updateZoomLevelDisplay() {\n   734→        const zoomDisplay = document.getElementById('zoom-level-display');\n   735→        if (zoomDisplay) {\n   736→            zoomDisplay.textContent = `${Math.round(this.zoomLevel * 100)}%`;\n   737→        }\n   738→    }\n   739→\n   740→    setupPanDrag() {\n   741→        const canvas = document.getElementById('image-canvas');\n   742→        const container = document.getElementById('canvas-container');\n   743→        if (!canvas || !container) return;\n   744→\n   745→        // Reset pan on new image load\n   746→        this.panX = 0;\n   747→        this.panY = 0;\n   748→\n   749→        // Update cursor style based on zoom level\n   750→        this.updateCanvasCursor();\n   751→\n   752→        // Mouse down - start dragging\n   753→        canvas.addEventListener('mousedown', (e) => {\n   754→            if (this.zoomLevel === 1.0) return; // Only allow panning when zoomed\n   755→\n   756→            this.isDragging = true;\n   757→            this.dragStartX = e.clientX;\n   758→            this.dragStartY = e.clientY;\n   759→        });\n   760→\n   761→        // Mouse move - update pan position\n   762→        canvas.addEventListener('mousemove', (e) => {\n   763→            if (!this.isDragging) return;\n   764→\n   765→            const dx = e.clientX - this.dragStartX;\n   766→            const dy = e.clientY - this.dragStartY;\n   767→\n   768→            this.panX += dx;\n   769→            this.panY += dy;\n   770→\n   771→            this.dragStartX = e.clientX;\n   772→            this.dragStartY = e.clientY;\n   773→\n   774→            this.reRenderCurrentImage();\n   775→        });\n   776→\n   777→        // Mouse up - stop dragging\n   778→        canvas.addEventListener('mouseup', () => {\n   779→            this.isDragging = false;\n   780→        });\n   781→\n   782→        // Mouse leave - stop dragging\n   783→        canvas.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => {\n   784→            this.isDragging = false;\n   785→        });\n   786→    }\n   787→\n   788→    updateCanvasCursor() {\n   789→        const canvas = document.getElementById('image-canvas');\n   790→        if (!canvas) return;\n   791→\n   792→        if (this.zoomLevel === 1.0) {\n   793→            canvas.classList.remove('zoomed');\n   794→        } else {\n   795→            canvas.classList.add('zoomed');\n   796→        }\n   797→    }\n   798→\n   799→    navigateImage(direction) {\n   800→        const items = document.querySelectorAll('.image-list-item');\n   801→        if (items.length === 0) return;\n   802→\n   803→        let currentIndex = -1;\n   804→        items.forEach((item, index) => {\n   805→            if (item.classList.contains('active')) {\n   806→                currentIndex = index;\n   807→            }\n   808→        });\n   809→\n   810→        if (currentIndex === -1) return;\n   811→\n   812→        let newIndex = currentIndex + direction;\n   813→        if (newIndex < 0) newIndex = items.length - 1;\n   814→        if (newIndex >= items.length) newIndex = 0;\n   815→\n   816→        const newImageId = parseInt(items[newIndex].getAttribute('data-image-id'));\n   817→        this.selectImage(newImageId);\n   818→    }\n   819→\n   820→    setupEventListeners() {\n   821→        // Load dataset button (navbar)\n   822→        const loadDatasetBtn = document.getElementById('btn-load-dataset');\n   823→        if (loadDatasetBtn) {\n   824→            loadDatasetBtn.addEventListener('click', () => this.showLoadDatasetModal());\n   825→        }\n   826→\n   827→        // Load dataset button (empty state)\n   828→        const emptyLoadDatasetBtn = document.getElementById('empty-load-dataset');\n   829→        if (emptyLoadDatasetBtn) {\n   830→            emptyLoadDatasetBtn.addEventListener('click', () => this.showLoadDatasetModal());\n   831→        }\n   832→\n   833→        // Load dataset button (comparison view)\n   834→    const tableBody = document.getElementById('metrics-table-body');\n   981→        if (!tableBody) return;\n   982→\n   983→        const rows = Array.from(tableBody.querySelectorAll('tr'));\n !cellB) return 0;\n   995→\n   996→            let valueA, valueB;\n   997→\n   998→            if (type === 'string') {\n   999→                valueA = cellA.textContent.trim().toLowerCase();\n  1000→                valueB = cellB.textContent.trim().toLowerCase();\n  1001→            } else {\n  1002→                // For numeric values, remove formatting and parse\n  1003→ 1007→            if (valueA < valueB) return direction === 'asc' ? -1 : 1;\n  1008→            if (valueA > valueB) return direction === 'asc' ? 1 : -1;\n  1009→            return 0;\n  1010→
